worldBankMetrics.label: General Government Expense (% GDP)
worldBankMetrics.definition: Consolidated general government expense as a share of GDP.

worldBankMetrics.label: General Government Final Consumption (% GDP)
worldBankMetrics.definition: Government final consumption only (narrower than total expense).

worldBankMetrics.label: Government Expense Per Capita (PPP)
worldBankMetrics.definition: Absolute spending scale per person at PPP prices.

usContextMetrics.label: Federal Net Outlays (% GDP)
usContextMetrics.definition: Federal-only net outlays share of GDP (excludes state and local).
notes: US-only federal scope; not an international general-government metric.

usContextMetrics.label: Federal + State/Local Current Expenditures (% GDP, derived)
usContextMetrics.definition: Non-consolidated current expenditure flow ratio for US fiscal context.
notes: US-only and not cross-country harmonized; useful as domestic context only.

usContextMetrics.label: Government Consumption + Investment (% GDP, derived)
notes: International analog exists, but it measures a narrower concept than total government expense.

rationale: GC.XPN.TOTL.GD.ZS is the broadest internationally harmonized spending-size series in the current pipeline.
It is already available for the full analysis panel and aligns with the current cross-country optimizer input.
Per-capita PPP companion metrics help separate real spending scale from GDP-ratio denominator effects.
caveats: Different accounting frameworks can still reduce comparability across countries.
Expense % GDP reflects both numerator policy changes and denominator (GDP) fluctuations.
US-only flow measures (federal outlays, federal+state/local current expenditures) are useful context but not panel-compatible defaults.
